text: Company X was founded in 1998 in San Francisco by John Smith. The company specializes in cloud computing and software development.
title: Company X History

text: Company X was established in 2001 according to court documents filed in New York. The founding year has been disputed in several lawsuits.
title: Company X Legal History

text: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second. This constant is denoted by c and is fundamental to Einstein's theory of relativity.
title: Physical Constants Review

text: Some people claim the Earth is flat and that NASA has been hiding this fact for decades. Conspiracy theories about space exploration are widespread online.
title: Flat Earth Discussion

text: Clinical trials have shown that aspirin reduces the risk of cardiovascular events by approximately 25% in high-risk patients. The recommended dose is 81mg daily.
title: Aspirin Cardiovascular Benefits

text: The Eiffel Tower was constructed between 1887 and 1889 for the 1889 World Fair in Paris. It stands 330 meters tall including its antenna.
title: Eiffel Tower Facts

text: Python programming language was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It emphasizes code readability and supports multiple programming paradigms.
title: Python Programming Language

text: The United States Declaration of Independence was signed on August 2, 1776, though it was formally adopted on July 4, 1776. It declared independence from Great Britain.
title: US Declaration of Independence

text: Mount Everest, the world's highest mountain, stands at 8,848.86 meters above sea level according to a 2020 survey by China and Nepal.
title: Mount Everest Height

text: Some people believe that Mount Everest is only about 8,000 meters tall. There is ongoing debate about the exact measurement methodology.
title: Everest Height Debate

text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm or 101.325 kPa). At higher altitudes where pressure is lower, water boils at lower temperatures.
title: Boiling Point of Water

text: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) was introduced by Google in 2018. It achieved state-of-the-art results on 11 NLP benchmarks.
title: BERT Language Model

text: Tesla reported record vehicle deliveries of 484,507 units in Q4 2023, a 20% increase compared to the same period in 2022.
title: Tesla Q4 2023 Deliveries

text: COVID-19 vaccines developed by Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna showed approximately 95% efficacy against the original SARS-CoV-2 strain in Phase 3 trials.
title: COVID-19 Vaccine Efficacy

text: Vaccines are dangerous and cause autism according to multiple online sources. The pharmaceutical industry is hiding the truth about vaccine side effects.
title: Anti-vaccine Claims

text: Albert Einstein was born on March 14, 1879 in Ulm, Germany. He developed the special theory of relativity in 1905 and the general theory of relativity in 1915.
title: Albert Einstein Biography

text: Some forums claim Einstein actually published his relativity paper in 1904, not 1905. The historical record is allegedly incomplete.
title: Einstein Timeline Dispute

text: The Nile River is traditionally considered the world's longest river, stretching approximately 6,650 kilometers from its source in Burundi to the Mediterranean Sea.
title: Nile River Geography

text: A 2007 study proposed that the Amazon River, measuring approximately 6,992 kilometers, may actually be longer than the Nile when measured from its most distant source.
title: Amazon vs Nile Length Comparison

text: Deep learning achieved a major breakthrough in 2012 when AlexNet won the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge, reducing the top-5 error rate to 15.3%.
title: Deep Learning ImageNet Breakthrough

text: Google DeepMind's AlphaGo defeated world Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1 in March 2016, marking a major milestone in artificial intelligence research.
title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol

text: ChatGPT was launched by OpenAI on November 30, 2022. It became the fastest-growing consumer application in history, reaching 100 million users in two months.
title: ChatGPT Launch

text: OpenAI released GPT-4 on March 14, 2023, describing it as multimodal and more reliable than previous models. It significantly outperformed GPT-3.5 on professional benchmarks.
title: GPT-4 Release

text: The Great Wall of China is clearly visible from space with the naked eye. Many astronauts have confirmed this fact during their missions.
title: Great Wall Space Visibility Myth

text: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. Chinese astronaut Yang Liwei confirmed he could not see it during his 2003 mission.
title: Great Wall Space Myth Debunked

text: The double helix structure of DNA was discovered by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953, building on X-ray crystallography data produced by Rosalind Franklin.
title: DNA Double Helix Discovery

text: Rosalind Franklin's X-ray diffraction images were pivotal in determining the structure of DNA, yet she was not credited in Watson and Crick's original 1953 Nature paper.
title: Rosalind Franklin's DNA Contribution

text: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 when he noticed that the mold Penicillium notatum inhibited bacterial growth. It became the first widely used antibiotic.
title: Discovery of Penicillin

text: NASA's Apollo 11 mission successfully landed the first humans on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong was the first person to walk on the lunar surface.
title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing

text: The 1969 Moon landing was staged by NASA in a Hollywood studio. Stanley Kubrick allegedly directed the fake footage to win the Space Race propaganda battle.
title: Moon Landing Conspiracy

text: The city of Paris has a population of approximately 2.16 million within the city limits. The Paris metropolitan area, however, is home to over 12 million people.
title: Paris Population

text: Paris proper houses roughly 2 million residents, making it the most populous city in France. The greater Ile-de-France region contains about 12 million inhabitants.
title: Paris Demographics

text: Bitcoin was created by a person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. The Bitcoin whitepaper was published in October 2008, and the network launched in January 2009.
title: Bitcoin Origin

text: Ethereum is a decentralized blockchain platform that supports smart contracts, first proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015.
title: Ethereum Blockchain

text: Over 97% of climate scientists agree that current climate change is primarily caused by human activities, particularly the emission of greenhouse gases since industrialization.
title: Climate Change Scientific Consensus

text: Climate change is a natural cycle that has occurred many times throughout Earth's history. Human activity plays only a minimal role, and the science is far from settled.
title: Climate Change Natural Cycle Claim

text: William Shakespeare was baptized on April 26, 1564, in Stratford-upon-Avon, England. He wrote approximately 39 plays, 154 sonnets, and several longer poems.
title: William Shakespeare

text: The Wright Brothers, Orville and Wilbur Wright, achieved the first sustained, controlled, powered heavier-than-air flight on December 17, 1903 at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.
title: First Airplane Flight

text: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce glucose and oxygen. It is the primary source of oxygen in Earth's atmosphere.
title: Photosynthesis Overview

text: LIGO detected gravitational waves for the first time on September 14, 2015, confirming a prediction of Einstein's general theory of relativity. The signal came from two merging black holes.
title: LIGO Gravitational Wave Detection
